description:
## Background
The Mermaid viewport integrates generated SVG and Panzoom through a direct React `useEffect`. This is a genuine external-system lifecycle, but it must use the explicit no-use-effect escape hatch or a component boundary.

## What to build
Replace the Mermaid viewport effect that mounts the generated SVG, creates Panzoom, installs the modified-wheel handler, and tears everything down when the SVG changes or the viewport unmounts.

## Technical Approach
Use the project `useMountEffect` escape hatch only if the viewport is remounted per SVG identity; otherwise introduce the smallest component/key boundary that gives each SVG its own mount lifecycle. Keep the imperative DOM boundary isolated and preserve fit-to-container initialization, zoom limits, wheel modifier behavior, and toolbar controls.

## Considerations
Cleanup must remove the wheel listener, destroy Panzoom, clear its ref, and remove the old SVG. Failed Panzoom initialization must not hide the already-rendered diagram or Source view. Avoid allowing an old SVG instance or handler to survive a source change.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The generated SVG remains visible and centered with the same fit transform and zoom controls.
- [ ] Modifier-wheel zoom behavior and Panzoom cleanup remain correct across source changes and unmounts.
- [ ] This lifecycle has no direct `useEffect` call and passes the configured Oxlint restriction.
- [ ] Focused Mermaid tests and relevant lint/typecheck checks pass.

## Test Plan
- Run the existing Mermaid desktop/E2E and component tests.
- Add regression coverage for source replacement and cleanup if needed.
- Run the relevant Oxlint and TypeScript checks.

Triage: ready-for-agent.
title: Replace Mermaid viewport pan/zoom lifecycle effect
